title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. algeriense
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. algeriense, also known as wild angelica, is a subspecies of biennial flowering plant in the carrot family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as coumarins and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of wild angelica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Wild angelica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems.
